sections.paragraph:
fut. יִשְׁקֹר. to lie, followed by a dat. of pers. to deceive any one, Gen 21:23. (The primary idea is perhaps that of colouring, compare شقر to be red, شُقْرَةُ red colour, paint, falsehood; see Tsepregi, in Diss. Lugdd. p. 115; compare the kindred שָׁרֹק.)

Piel, to lie, 1Sa 15:29 followed by בְּ of pers. Lev 19:11 also, בְּ of thing, to deceive; שִׁקֵּר בִּבְרִית to deceive in a covenant, i.e. perfidiously to break a covenant, Psa 44:18 בֶּאֱמוּנָה to be false to one’s faith, Psa 89:34 without an acc. id. Isa 63:8.
